pattern.name: Beholder
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)

items.name: Eye Colors:
entries: Slime, Pale Yellow, Cyan, Varsity Orange, Magenta, small amounts

notions: 12-gauge armature wire (copper wire from a hardware store works great), 17 feet / 5 m
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.
notes: This pattern is worked in continuous rounds unless otherwise noted.

items: {@b (MAKE 10)}
Cut 20-in. / 51-cm lengths of wire for eyestalks.

Using the needle-nose pliers, curl one end in on itself to protect yourself from injury. Then curl the end in a small spiral (use about 2 in. / 5 cm of wire to make the curl) to fit in the top of the Eyestalk at the eyeball.
{@b (MAKE 10, 2 IN EACH EYE COLOR)}
With {@b Eye Color}, make a magic ring.
Change to {@b A}.
Change to {@b MC}.

items.name: Rnd 7:
entries: Sc around in both loops of the current round as well as the unworked blo from Rnd 4. (18)

items: At this point, attach a 12-mm safety eye in the magic ring and secure.
In the following rounds, add the armature wire and polyester fiberfill as you go.

Place the spiral of the wire against the back of the eye. It helps to use the tails from the color changes to tie the wire spiral into place. Stuff firmly to keep the wire in place and continue to stuff firmly as you work.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. You should have about 3 in. / 8 cm of wire showing from the end of the Eyestalk.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Flatten the Eyelid into a half circle. Slst the opening closed. Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Set aside until you are finished with the Head/Body.
With {@b B}, make a magic ring.
Pull up a loop, bring {@b B} forward, do not cut off. Attach {@b A} in next st.
Pull up a loop, send {@b A} to the back and put your hook into {@b B}.
Slst with {@b B} in the spaces between {@b B} and {@b A} all the way around. (60)
Fasten off {@b B}.

items.name: Rnd 13:
entries: Pick {@b A} up again, 5sc, inc, [10sc, inc] × 5, 5sc (72)

items: Place the 24-mm safety eye in the center of the magic ring and secure into place.
Change to {@b MC}.

items.name: Rnd 22:
entries: Work in both the regular loops as well as the unworked blo from Rnd 19, 7sc, inc, [14sc, inc] × 5, 7sc. (96)

items: Now we will be making the Mouth opening.

items.name: Rnd 27:
entries: [19sc, inc] × 2, 8sc, ch1, turn, dec, 4sc, dec, ch1, turn, dec, 2sc, dec. Fasten off.

items: Sk24, attach yarn, 8sc, ch1, turn, dec, 4sc, dec, ch1, turn, dec, 2sc, dec, 2sc evenly down the side of the cheek you just made, make dec in the base of the cheek and the next st, 18sc, inc, 19sc, inc (118 visible sc stitches)

items.name: Rnd 28:
entries: 10sc, inc, 20sc, inc, 10sc, 2sc evenly up the side of the cheek, 4sc across the top of the cheek, ch38, skip the Mouth, begin at the top of the other cheek, 16sc, inc, 20sc, inc, 10sc (138)

items: Right now we are going to pause here so we can work on the Mouth. Insert a stitch marker in the loop to save your place.
With {@b MC}, we are now going to work on the inside of the Mouth.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Attach yarn to the bottom corner of the Mouth. 38sc across the bottom of the Mouth, 4sc up the cheek, 24sc across the top of the Mouth, 4sc down the other cheek. (70)

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: In regular loops and unworked blo from Rnd 2, 38sc, 2dec, 24sc, 2dec (66)

items: Change to C.
Slst, fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Push the Mouth to the inside of the Head/Body and squeeze the inside of the Mouth closed so that it is evenly flat across the Mouth. From the inside of the Head/Body, sew the Mouth closed.
Now make the Teeth.
With {@b A}, attach yarn in a space between the {@b MC} and {@b C}.
[Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, 2slst, sc, sk st in the Mouth, 2slst] repeat until you have worked all the way around the Mouth.
Fasten off, bring yarn tails to the inside of the Head/Body, and weave in ends.
{@b (CONTINUED)}
Now that the Mouth is complete, let's finish the Head/Body. Remove stitch marker and continue as follows.
Place a stitch marker in your loop. We will now attach 5 of the Eyestalks.

Get 1 Eyestalk in each color. Eyestalks will go somewhere between Rnds 39 and 55. Place 1 Eyestalk above the main eye, slightly off center. Poke the wire into the Head/Body and use your pliers to curl the end of the wire and bend it flush with the inside of the Head/Body. Pin the Eyestalk in place and sew. Be sure to stuff the base of the Eyestalk completely as you sew it in place.

Continue placing the next 4 Eyestalks in a kind of headband, but do not place any below the corners of the Mouth. They do not need to be equidistant from each other. Once the first 5 Eyestalks are in place, curl them toward the face to make it easier to continue working on the rest of the Head/Body.

Place a stitch marker in your loop. We will now attach the rest of the Eyestalks. Attach the Eyestalks in the same manner as before, placing them behind and offset from the first 5 stalks. Try not to place 2 of the same color eyes next to each other. Curl the Eyestalks toward the face and continue working on the rest of the Head/Body.

Begin to stuff by placing the dried beans in a nylon stocking or another fabric bag and place in the bottom of the Head/Body. Stuff firmly with the polyester fiberfill to hold the bag in place. Be sure to stuff around the Mouth and push the polyester fiberfill around until you fill the area well. Stuff the eye very firmly so it can keep its shape. Continue to stuff firmly as you close up the Head/Body.
Fasten off, sew opening closed, and weave in ends.

Now attach the Eyelid. Pin the slst edge along the top of the main eye, just to the outside of the ridge we made in {@b MC}. Sew into place. If you like, you can add a bit of hot glue under the Eyelid to hold it in place.

pattern.name: Displacer Beast
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value Pound Plus} (100% acrylic, 1093 yd. / 1000 m per 17.6 oz / 500 g skein)

items.name: Additional Colors:
entries: Small amounts of worsted-weight (#4 medium) yarn in Pink, Cream, and Black

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: Puff Stitch (puff):
entries: Yarn over, insert hook and pull up a loop 5 times, yo and pull through all loops on hook and ch1. To work the next row above the puff, work in the ch1 space.

items.name: Double Puff Stitch (dblpuff):
entries: Yarn over, insert hook into first space and pull up a loop 2 times, yo and insert hook into next space 3 times, yo and insert hook into next space 2 times, yo and pull through all loops on the hook, and ch1. To work the next row above the dblpuff, work in the ch1 space. You are decreasing when you make this stitch.

items.name: Popcorn Stitch (popcorn):
entries: 5dc in stitch indicated, remove hook from loop, insert hook into first dc made, grab loop and pull through, ch1 to finish. When working in the top of the popcorn, work in the ch1.

notes: Each popcorn stitch (see above) is considered one stitch for the total stitch count at the end of each round. Carry the colors with you instead of cutting at every color change.

items: With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Sc, slst, fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Insert safety eyes between Rnds 21 and 22, 15 spaces apart between each eye post.
Begin stuffing the Head firmly and continue stuffing as you go.
Fasten off, sew hole closed, and weave in the tail.
Pin Muzzle to the Head—the bridge of the nose will be in between the eyes. Stuff as you pin. Sew in place and stuff firmly as you sew.
With {@b Black}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch5, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, 4sc (4)

items: Fasten off, leave a long tail for sewing.
Pin the Nose to the Muzzle. Sew into place.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch13, beginning in 2nd ch from hook sc, hdc, 8dc, hdc, sc, ch13, beginning in 2nd ch from hook sc, hdc, 8dc, hdc, sc (24)

items: This makes 2 Eyelids that are connected.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.
Pin the points and flat edge around the eyes first, then pull the edges to open the Eyelids around the eyes. Sew into place.

entries.name: Outside Ears
items: With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, 3sc in next, sc (5)

items: Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Inside Ears
items: With {@b Pink}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, 3sc in next, sc (5)

items: With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Ch1, turn, stack the Inside Ear on top of the Outside Ear, working through both pieces, 10sc, 3sc in next, 10sc (23)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Ch1 and work 20sc evenly across the bottom of the Ear, slst (20)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Weave in all other ends.
Curve the Ears and pin to the top of the Head. Sew in place.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Now you will work in rows.

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Ch1, turn, dec, 5sc, dec, sc in the side of Row 11, dec on Rnd 10, 5sc, dec, sc in the side of Row 11 (16)

items: Now the first st of the round is the first dec from Row 12.
Stuff the Foot and Leg as you work.

As you finish the top of the Leg around the shoulder don't stuff it quite so full. It needs to be somewhat flattened when it gets attached to the Body. However, the rest of the Leg still needs to be stuffed firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. Sew hole closed, leaving the tail so you can attach the Leg to the Body.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Now you will work in rows.

items.name: Row 10:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 11:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items.name: Row 13:
entries: Turn, sk slst, 6sc, slst in the next unworked st from Rnd 7 (6)

items: Now you will work in rounds.
Stuff the Foot and Leg as you work.

As you finish the top of the Leg around the shoulder, don't stuff it quite so full. It needs to be somewhat flattened when it gets attached to the Body. However, the rest of the Leg still needs to be stuffed firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail. Sew hole closed, leaving the tail so you can attach the Leg to the Body.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Ch7, beginning in 2nd ch from hook 5sc, 2sc in last ch, working on the backside of the ch, 5sc (12)

items: Begin stuffing, and continue stuffing as you go.
Fasten off and sew hole closed, leaving a long tail for sewing.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Mark the center decrease with a stitch marker.
Mark the center decrease with a stitch marker.
The side with the three decreases is the front of the Haunch—basically, the knee.
Stuff lightly, you will be flattening the Haunch against the Body.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

Pin the Haunch to the Back Foot/Leg with the knee facing toward the toes and the back of the Haunch lined up with the back of the Foot. The bottom of the Haunch should be slightly opened so it can be sewn in an oval shape to the top of the Foot. Use the tail from the Foot to sew the Haunch into place. Save the tail from the Haunch to sew the Back Foot/Leg to the Body.

instructions.name: Tentacles
entries: Cut two 25-in. / 64-cm lengths of wire. With pliers bend ½ in. / 1 cm of one end over to keep the point from poking through the material. Now, make a complete bend 5 in. / 12.5 cm from the same end, forming a really long U-shape. Curve the 2 sides of the U outward to make a petal-like shape for the Tentacle Heads. Continue to adjust these as you assemble your Tentacle Heads.

entries.name: Inner Tentacle Head
items: With {@b Pink}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, sc across (3)

items.name: Row 2:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc (3)

items.name: Row 4:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc, switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc (5)

items.name: Row 6:
entries: Ch1, turn, sc, [switch to {@b Cream}, popcorn, switch to {@b Pink}, sc] × 3 (7)

items: Now you can cut the {@b Cream} color.

items.name: Row 18:
entries: Ch1, turn, 2sc, 3sc in last st, turn and 15sc evenly down the side, (3sc, sc, 3sc) along the bottom, 15sc evenly up the side, 2sc in same st as first sc of this row, slst (44)

items: Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Outer Tentacle Head
items: With {@b MC}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch4, beginning in 2nd ch from hook, sc across (3)

items.name: Row 18:
entries: Ch1, turn, 2sc, 3sc in last st, turn and 15sc evenly down the side, (3sc, sc, 3sc) along the bottom, 15sc evenly up the side, 2sc in same st as first sc of this row, slst (44)

items: Do not fasten off.

entries.name: Assembling Tentacles
items: Sandwich the wrong sides of the Inner and Outer Tentacle Heads together. Make sure the tops of each piece match up. Use stitch markers to hold everything together while you work around both pieces. Do NOT place the wire yet.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch1, turn, 43sc evenly around both Heads, stuffing as you work (43; you should have 2 stitches left unworked)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing. Lay the wire in place around the Tentacle Heads and with pliers shape it to fit. Fold the flap you just made over the wire and sew into place along the seam where {@b Pink} meets {@b MC}. When you get to the end, sew the two sides together with one little stitch. You've now created the head piece for the end of the stalk of the Tentacle. Fasten off, weave in ends.

entries.name: Tentacle Stem

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Leave a long beginning tail and attach {@b MC} anywhere along the bottom of the Tentacle Head. 12sc evenly around the {@b MC} part only (do not work in the Pink part). Leave the long tail hanging out in case you need to stitch up any gaping holes. (12)

items: Stuff and continue stuffing as you work.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
Cut an 18-in. / 46-cm length of wire. Using pliers, bend about ½ in. / 1 cm over on itself to keep the end from poking through the tip of the Tail.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Insert the bent end of the wire into the start of the Tail and stuff around it as you work. Use the yarn tail to hold the wire in place.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.
With {@b MC}, make a magic ring.
Place a stitch marker in the loop.

items.name: Attach the Tail to the Body:
entries: Poke the wire of the Tail between Rnds 8 and 9, directly below the first st of the round. Using pliers, wrap the end of the wire into a spiral, then fold flat against the fabric inside the Body. Pin the Tail, and sew in place. This will make the Body lean slightly forward. This is fine; the Legs will stabilize the Body once you attach them. Feel free to curl the Tail up into an S-shape. This will help keep it out of the way while you work.

items: Begin stuffing the Body firmly.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail for sewing.

items.name: Attach the Tentacles:
entries: First determine where the center of the back of the neck is, using the center of the Tail as a reference point. Insert a stitch marker to identify the area. Insert the wire from the Tentacles between Rnds 63 and 54, with about 7 stitches between the wires. Using pliers, curl the wire into a spiral and bend the spiral to flatten against the fabric inside the Body. Pin the Tentacles in place, making sure that the Tentacle Heads face toward the neck opening, and sew. Stuff the ends of the Tentacles completely as you sew.

items: Finish stuffing the Body firmly, as much as possible, up to the neck.

items.name: Back Legs:
entries: Brace the Body in an upright position, leaning forward so that the Tail isn't squished under the Body. Press the Haunch slightly into the foot so that it leans forward just a bit. Pin the Back Legs onto the Body with the back of the Haunches flattened completely from the back of the foot up to the beginning round, one or two stitches away from either side of the Tail. Leave the front of the Haunches unpinned. Press the Back Legs toward the Body and pin in place. This will help keep the Body from tipping forward. Use the yarn tail from the back of the foot to sew the Haunch and Back Legs in place.

items.name: Middle Legs:
entries: With the Body standing on the Back Legs, press the Middle Legs into the floor and against the back foot. There should be as little space as possible between the two. Press the top of the Middle Leg flat against the Body and pin in place. Sew the Middle Leg around the shoulder and tack the inner part of the Middle Leg to the Body to keep it from flaring out.

items.name: Front Legs:
entries: Pin the Front Legs just above the Middle Legs, with the shoulder completely flattened against the Body. Sew into place, only around the back curve of the shoulder, with each foot in a slightly different raised position to give the appearance that the beast is about to jump after its Tentacles. If the foot wants to flare out, you can tack it to the Body with a hidden stitch or two.

items.name: Attach the Head to the Body:
entries: Make sure to stuff the neck well as you sew the Body to the Head to keep it from flopping forward.

pattern.name: Gelatinous Cube
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.
notes: The body is primarily worked in continuous rounds. The Arrow, Sword, Skull, Eyes, and Eyebrows are worked separately and then attached.
Stitches in parentheses are all worked in the same space.

items: With {@b A}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 9:
entries: 14hdc, 3hdc in corner space, [15hdc, 3hdc in corner space] × 3; slst in the next stitch and ch1 (71)

items.name: Rnd 10:
entries: Starting in the same stitch as the slst made at the end of Rnd 9, all in blo—3sc, 2dc, hdc, 3sc, dc, tr, dc, hdc, 4sc

items: (Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 3sc, hdc, dc, hdc, 5sc
(Ch5, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 2sc), 2sc
(Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 2sc, hdc, dc, hdc, 3sc, 2dc, hdc, 4sc
(Ch3, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc), 5sc
(Ch7, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 4sc), 4sc, dc, 2tr, dc, hdc, 5sc
(Ch5, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 2sc), 3sc
(Ch8, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 2hdc, 5sc), 3sc, fasten off with a long yarn tail

items.name: Step 1:
entries: Using the Top of the Head as a guide, cut a piece of plastic canvas slightly smaller than the Top of the Head. It should reach the midway point of Rnd 9.

items.name: Step 2:
entries: Cut 5 more plastic canvas squares the same size as the one just measured to make the Body Structure and set aside for later.

items.name: Step 3:
entries: With a long piece of yarn in {@b A} to match the Top of the Head, attach the plastic canvas squares to the bottom side of the Top of the Head square.

items.name: Step 4:
entries: With a long piece of yarn in {@b B} to match the Body, sew the 5 plastic canvas squares together in a cube shape. Leave the top side of the cube open and stuff with polyester fiberfill. Set the Body Structure aside and continue to the Body crochet section.

items: Flip the Top of the Head over so the bottom side with the plastic canvas is face up. Insert the crochet hook into the back bump of one of the hdc stitches of Row 9 on the Head and attach {@b B}.
{@i Note: The crochet hook should be inserted going toward the middle of the Head.}

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Working in the back bump of each hdc stitch of Rnd 9, [7sc, dec] × 8. (64)

items: Insert the plastic canvas cube into the Body—be sure to stuff the cube with polyfill before inserting. Add or remove rows, as needed, if the sides of the Body do not reach the end of the plastic canvas cube.

items.name: Rnd 21:
entries: Slst, and ch1 at the end of Rnd 20. Starting in the same stitch as the slst [6sc blo, dec] × 8. (56)

items: Fasten off and use the yarn tail to close the remaining 8 stitches. Weave in ends.
Fold down Rnd 10 of the Top of the Head across the top of the Body and sew into place. Fasten off and weave in any ends.
{@b (MAKE 2)}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: With {@b A}, ch7, starting in 2nd ch from the hook, 6sc. Fasten off with a long yarn tail. (6)

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b D}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: 4sc in ring. Change to {@b A} and slst, ch1 at the start of the row. (4)

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: 4inc blo. Change to {@b E} and slst, ch1 at the start of the row. (8)

items.name: Rnd 3:
entries: Blo [inc, sc] × 4. Slst and fasten off with a long yarn tail. (12)

items: With {@b E}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 11:
entries: 2sc, dec, 2sc, 5inc flo, 2sc. Fasten off with a long yarn tail and stuff with polyester fiberfill. (17)

items: Flatten the bottom of the Skull and sew closed with yarn tail. With {@b D}, sew X-shaped eyes over Rnd 7 with 3 stitches between and an upside down V-shaped nose on Rnd 9, centered between the eyes.
{@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b G}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off with a long tail.
With {@b C}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

items.name: Assemble the top half of the Arrow:
entries: Insert the closed side of Arrow Shaft into the bottom of the Arrowhead. Flatten the Arrowhead and sew closed.

items: With {@b E}

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Ch13, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook—inc, 3sc, 2dec, 3sc, inc, ch1 and turn (12)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail.

items.name: Assemble the bottom half of the Arrow:
entries: Lay the closed side of the Arrow Shaft over the top of the Arrow Fletching. Using the tail end from the Arrow Fletching, sew both pieces together.

items: With {@b F}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 4:
entries: [Sc, dec] × 3, change to {@b G} at the end of the row (6)

items.name: Rnds 5–9:
entries: 6sc, change to {@b F} at the end of Rnd 9 (6)

items.name: Rnd 12:
entries: 4sc, inc, sc, inc, 6sc, inc, sc, inc, 2sc (22)

items: Fasten off with a long yarn tail.
With {@b C}, make a magic ring.
Fasten off with a long yarn tail.

items.name: Assemble the Sword:
entries: Insert the closed side of the Blade into the Sword Hilt and fold the gold piece flat across the Blade. Sew the Hilt closed using the yarn tail from the Hilt.

items.name: Eyes and Eyebrows:
entries: Pick a side of the Cube as the front. Attach the Eyes between Rnds 10 and 15. Attach the Eyebrows above the Eyes, just covering the top inner corners of the Eyes and angled downward in an angry expression.

items.name: Skull:
entries: Attach to the top left corner of the Cube's Head.

items.name: Top Half of Arrow:
entries: Attach to the right side of the Cube with the Arrow pointing downward.

items.name: Bottom Half of Arrow:
entries: Attach to the left side of the Cube with the Fletching pointing upward. Line up the Arrow top and bottom to appear as if it were going through the Cube.

items.name: Sword:
entries: Attach to the top right corner of the Cube's Head.

items.name: Mouth:
entries: Using a 12 in. / 30.5 cm length of {@b D} and a tapestry needle, stitch a mouth just below the Eyes between Rnds 17 and 18 in a )—( shape.

pattern.name: Mind Flayer
yarn: Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Red Heart {@i Super Saver} (100% acrylic, 364 yd. / 333 m per 7 oz. / 198 g skein)
Worsted weight (#4 medium) yarn, shown in Big Twist {@i Value} (100% acrylic, 380 yd. / 348 m per 6 oz. / 170 g skein)

items.name: Color D (belt, chest, and shoulder armor):

gauge: Gauge is not critical for this project. Make sure your stitches are tight so the stuffing won't show through.

items.name: Surface Slip Stitch (surface slst):
entries: Like a regular slip stitch, but instead of working into the edge, insert your hook into the front surface of the fabric to create a visible line.

notes: The project is worked primarily in single crochet stitches in the amigurumi style. The Cape is worked separately and attached.
Stitches in parentheses are worked into the same space.

items: With {@b B}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 13:
entries: This round creates the Tentacles—four total. 6sc, [sc flo, ch 12, starting in 2nd ch from hook: [inc, sc] × 4, 3sc] × 4, sc flo, 3sc. (24)

items.name: Rnd 14:
entries: 24sc. The sc stitches behind the Tentacles go in the unworked back loops of the previous round. (24)

items: Insert 12-mm safety eyes between Rnds 11 and 12 with 5 spaces between. Stuff the Head medium firm with polyester fiberfill.
Fasten off and close up the last 8 stitches with the yarn tail. Weave in ends.
With {@b B}, embroider eyebrows just above the eyes between Rnds 9 and 11. Angle the eyebrows downward for a scowling face.

items.name: Optional:
entries: To shape the eyes, use the yarn tail to sew back and forth around the outer sides of the eyes and pull tight until the eyes are sunken into a preferred depth.

items: {@b (MAKE 2)}
With {@b B}, make a magic ring.

items.name: Rnd 2:
entries: Sc, [sc (ch5, starting in the 2nd ch from the hook 2slst, 2sc)] × 4, sc (6)

items: Change to {@b D}.

items.name: Rnd 7:
entries: Sc in each stitch by working through the blo of the slst in the previous round and through the sc stitch of Rnd 5 (9)

items: Change to {@b A}.

items.name: Rnd 9:
entries: Sc in each stitch by working through the blo of the slst in the previous round and through the sc stitch of Rnd 7 (9)

items: Stuff the Arm medium firm with polyester fiberfill.

items.name: Rnd 15:
entries: Fold in half and 4sc across the folded stitches, working through both sides (4)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail to attach to the Body Base.
With {@b A}, ch12.

items.name: Rnd 1:
entries: Join the ch stitches into a loop by making a sc in the first ch stitch of the round. Sc in remaining ch spaces. (12)

items: Stuff the Body Base medium firm with polyester fiberfill.
Fasten off and close up the last 6 stitches with yarn tail. Weave in ends.

Attach {@b A} to the last stitch of Rnd 12 on the Body Base. The first row is worked in the open front loop spaces. The Body Base should be upside down and your crochet hook should be inserted with the tip pointed toward the bottom of the Body Base.

items.name: Row 12:
entries: Working into the sc stitches of Row 11 and the open front loop spaces of Rnd 23 on the Body Base, [4sc, in the 5th stitch space insert hook into the same front loop space as the last stitch and into the next stitch over on Row 11] × 6, ch1 and turn. There should be 6 remaining front loop spaces that are unworked. (30)

items.name: Row 13:
entries: [4sc, inc] × 6, fasten off with a short yarn tail (36)

items.name: Row 14:
entries: Attach {@b D} to the starting sc of Row 13, [inc, 5sc] × 6, ch1 and do not turn (42)

items.name: Row 15:
entries: Working up the side of the Body Robe, sc approximately 13 stitches. 2sc in the front loop spaces on the Body Base (you will work these stitches next to the sc stitches from Row 1 that are already there). Working down the other side of the Body Robe, sc approximately 13 stitches. Slst at the starting sc of Row 14, fasten off, and weave in ends. (28)

items: With {@b D},

Insert crochet hook between Rows 12 and 13 of the Body Robe and surface slst around the Body. The surface slst spaces above the center of the Robe opening are worked through the {@b D} sc spaces at the top of the opening. Fasten off and weave in any ends.
With {@b D}, ch3.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 2sc, ch1 and turn (2)

items.name: Row 3:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 3sc, ch1 and turn (3)

items.name: Row 5:
entries: Starting in the 2nd ch from the hook, 4sc, ch1 and turn (4)

items.name: Row 6:
entries: 3sc, ch1 and turn—do not work a stitch in the last space (3)

items.name: Row 8:
entries: 2sc, ch1 and turn—do not work a stitch in the last space (2)

items: Fasten off, leaving a long tail.
With {@b C}, ch7.

items.name: Row 1:
entries: Starting in 2nd ch from the hook 6sc, ch1 and turn (6)

items: Fasten off, leaving a short tail.
With {@b C}, attach yarn to the starting chain row of the Cape Top with a slst.

items.name: Row 26:
entries: Sc approximately 32 stitches up the side of the Cape. Inc, 16sc, inc across the top of the Cape. Working down the other side of the Cape, sc approximately 32 stitches, slst into the starting sc from Row 25. Fasten off and weave in any ends.

items.name: Arms:
entries: Attach to the Body between Rnds 3 and 6, angled forward.

items.name: Armor:
entries: Pin the Chest and Shoulder Armor in place around the Chest and Arms. The middle point should sit between Rnds 3 and 6 of the Body and the side points should sit on top of the Arms. The start and end 2sc rows should be connected together to form the piece into one loop.

items.name: Head:
entries: Attach the Head to the top of the Body at the opening. The Head is slightly angled and attached between Rnds 2 and 4 of the Head.

items.name: Cape:
entries: Center the Cape Top on the back of the Head and attach between Rows 2 and 4 of the Cape Top and Rnds 11 and 14 of the Head. Attach the section of the Cape where the Cape Top and Bottom are connected to the base of the neck (where it connects to the Head).
